title: Galileo Galilei
englishTitle: Galileo — The Man Who Moved the Earth

lines.italian: Galileo è un uomo curioso.
lines.english: Galileo is a curious man.

lines.italian: Vive a Pisa, in Italia.
lines.english: He lives in Pisa, in Italy.

lines.italian: Guarda le cose. Fa domande.
lines.english: He looks at things. He asks questions.

lines.italian: "Perché le cose cadono?" chiede Galileo.
lines.english: "Why do things fall?" asks Galileo.

lines.italian: Va sulla Torre di Pisa.
lines.english: He goes to the Tower of Pisa.

lines.italian: Butta due palle. Una grande e una piccola.
lines.english: He drops two balls. A big one and a small one.

lines.italian: Cadono insieme! Alla stessa velocità!
lines.english: They fall together! At the same speed!

lines.italian: "Aristotele aveva torto," dice Galileo.
lines.english: "Aristotle was wrong," says Galileo.

lines.italian: Nessuno crede a Galileo.
lines.english: Nobody believes Galileo.

lines.italian: "Aristotele è il grande maestro. Tu sei nessuno."
lines.english: "Aristotle is the great master. You are nobody."

lines.italian: Ma Galileo non si ferma.
lines.english: But Galileo does not stop.

lines.italian: Un giorno, sente parlare di un nuovo strumento.
lines.english: One day, he hears about a new instrument.

lines.italian: Si chiama telescopio. Fa vedere le cose lontane.
lines.english: It is called a telescope. It makes distant things visible.

lines.italian: Galileo costruisce il suo telescopio. Più grande. Più forte.
lines.english: Galileo builds his own telescope. Bigger. Stronger.

lines.italian: La notte, guarda il cielo.
lines.english: At night, he looks at the sky.

lines.italian: Vede la luna. Non è liscia. Ha montagne e valli.
lines.english: He sees the moon. It is not smooth. It has mountains and valleys.

lines.italian: Vede Giove. Ha quattro lune!
lines.english: He sees Jupiter. It has four moons!

lines.italian: Vede Venere. Cambia forma come la luna.
lines.english: He sees Venus. It changes shape like the moon.

lines.italian: "La Terra gira intorno al Sole," dice Galileo.
lines.english: "The Earth goes around the Sun," says Galileo.

lines.italian: "Non è il Sole che gira intorno alla Terra."
lines.english: "It is not the Sun that goes around the Earth."

lines.italian: La Chiesa dice: "No! La Terra è al centro!"
lines.english: The Church says: "No! The Earth is at the center!"

lines.italian: "La Bibbia lo dice. Tu sbagli."
lines.english: "The Bible says so. You are wrong."

lines.italian: Galileo scrive un libro. Spiega le sue scoperte.
lines.english: Galileo writes a book. He explains his discoveries.

lines.italian: La Chiesa è arrabbiata.
lines.english: The Church is angry.

lines.italian: "Vieni a Roma. Devi parlare con noi."
lines.english: "Come to Rome. You must speak with us."

lines.italian: Galileo va a Roma. Ha settant'anni.
lines.english: Galileo goes to Rome. He is seventy years old.

lines.italian: "Dì che la Terra non si muove," dice la Chiesa.
lines.english: "Say that the Earth does not move," says the Church.

lines.italian: Galileo è vecchio e stanco.
lines.english: Galileo is old and tired.

lines.italian: "La Terra non si muove," dice Galileo.
lines.english: "The Earth does not move," says Galileo.

lines.italian: Ma poi dice piano: "Eppur si muove."
lines.english: But then he says quietly: "And yet it moves."

lines.italian: Galileo resta in casa sua per il resto della vita.
lines.english: Galileo stays in his house for the rest of his life.

lines.italian: Non può uscire. Non può insegnare.
lines.english: He cannot go out. He cannot teach.

lines.italian: Ma continua a pensare. Continua a scrivere.
lines.english: But he keeps thinking. He keeps writing.

lines.italian: Galileo muore nel 1642.
lines.english: Galileo dies in 1642.

lines.italian: Aveva ragione. La Terra si muove.
lines.english: He was right. The Earth moves.

lines.italian: Oggi tutti lo sanno.
lines.english: Today everyone knows it.
